name: Flipkart Product Description Normalizer
scenario: Flipkart processes 10M product titles daily. Current code uses String += concatenation (O(n²)) causing timeouts. Rebuild the pipeline using StringBuilder for all string operations.

core.description: normalize(String title): remove non-alphanumeric except spaces, collapse multiple spaces, trim, lowercase. Must use StringBuilder. O(n) time. Test: 'Apple  iPhone!!  15 Pro' → 'apple iphone 15 pro'.
reverseWords(String s): reverse word order in title. StringBuilder-based. O(n). Test: 'the sky is blue' → 'blue is sky the'.
runLengthEncode(String s): 'aaabbc' → 'a3b2c1'. O(n) StringBuilder. Test multiple inputs.
frequencyRank(String s): return top 3 most frequent characters as 'c1:count1, c2:count2, c3:count3'. int[26] frequency array.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): normalize correct, reverseWords correct, runLengthEncode correct, frequencyRank returns top-3 correctly.
README: explains why String += is O(n²) and StringBuilder is O(n) with concrete numbers.

bonus.description: benchmark(int n): time String concat vs StringBuilder for n random chars. Show ratio. n=50000.
decodeRunLength(String encoded): 'a3b2c1' → 'aaabbc'. Inverse of runLengthEncode. Verify roundtrip.
zigzagConvert(String s, int numRows): O(n) StringBuilder per row. Test PAYPALISHIRING rows=3 → PAHNAPLSIIGYIR.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. StringBuilder.append() is amortized O(1). String += in loop is O(n²). Pattern for frequency: int[26] freq; for(char c: s.toCharArray()) freq[c-'a']++. O(1) per char, O(1) space. reverseWords: s.trim().split('\\s+') splits on any whitespace including multiple spaces — avoids empty strings. StringBuilder.reverse() is O(n) — use it for reversal. String comparison: always .equals() never ==. charAt(i) is O(1). substring(l,r) is O(r-l) — avoid calling in tight loops.
